name: Gas Turbine Control System Attack
description: Compromise the Distributed Control System (DCS) managing gas turbines in a power generation plant to cause turbine overspeed, compressor surge, or forced shutdown — disrupting megawatts of generation capacity.
root_objective: Attack gas turbine DCS to cause turbine damage, forced trips, or generation disruption

nodes.title: Cause Gas Turbine Damage or Forced Trip via DCS Compromise
nodes.description: Gain control of the turbine DCS (e.g., GE Mark VIe, Siemens T3000, Emerson Ovation) to manipulate combustion parameters, disable protective trips, or force rapid load changes causing mechanical damage.

nodes.title: Compromise DCS Engineering Workstation
nodes.description: Gain access to the DCS engineering workstation to modify turbine control logic, setpoints, or alarm configurations.

nodes.title: Attack Safety Instrumented System (SIS)
nodes.description: Compromise the independent Safety Instrumented System (SIS) that provides turbine Emergency Shutdown (ESD) to prevent it from intervening during the attack.

nodes.title: Spearphish Plant Control Engineer
nodes.description: Target a control systems engineer with phishing to install a RAT on the corporate workstation, then pivot to the DCS engineering VLAN.

nodes.title: Exploit DCS Proprietary Protocol Vulnerabilities
nodes.description: Exploit known CVEs in the DCS vendor's proprietary protocol stack (e.g., GE SRTP, Siemens S7comm) to gain code execution on DCS controllers.

nodes.title: Modify Turbine Speed Governor Setpoints
nodes.description: Alter the speed governor logic to allow the turbine to exceed safe RPM limits, or modify fuel valve control curves to cause combustion instability.

nodes.title: Exploit SIS Engineering Workstation Connectivity
nodes.description: Access the SIS engineering laptop/workstation that connects to the Triconex/HIMA/Yokogawa safety controller for maintenance — typically only connected periodically.

nodes.title: Deploy TRITON-Style SIS Malware
nodes.description: Upload malicious safety logic to the SIS controller that disables Emergency Shutdown (ESD) interlocks while maintaining the appearance of normal operation.

nodes.title: Manipulate DCS Historian Data
nodes.description: Modify historical trend data in the PI/OPC historian to conceal the gradual parameter drift that precedes the attack, preventing operators from noticing anomalies.

nodes.title: DCS Controllers Run Legacy Firmware
nodes.description: Turbine DCS controllers run firmware versions with known vulnerabilities. Patching requires turbine outage and vendor coordination, so updates lag years behind.

nodes.title: TRITON Framework Adapted to Target SIS Vendor
nodes.description: Attacker must adapt the TRITON/TRISIS toolset or develop equivalent capability for the specific SIS vendor (Triconex, HIMA, or Yokogawa).

nodes.title: Data Historian Bridges Corporate and DCS Networks
nodes.description: The OSIsoft PI or Honeywell PHD historian server bridges the corporate IT and DCS control networks, providing a pivot point accessible from the phished engineer's workstation.
